checks.a_analysis: The output text accurately reflects the headings and primary list items found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or sections were identified.
No fabricated contact information (URLs, phone numbers, etc.) was found. While some URLs present in the source are missing from the output, no new or incorrect information has been added.
All primary sections of the agenda are present in the output. No entire pages or major top-level sections were omitted.
The source document does not contain any tables; the content is structured as lists and headers.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture almost all nested list items (sub-points) throughout the document. This results in significant information loss in multiple sections, exceeding the 25% threshold for those sections. Missing items include details for Elevation, Administrative/Governmental Units, NC Board on Geographic Names (specifically the list of names for approval), and the Metadata Committee update. Additionally, the footer containing URLs and the 'Join meeting' link text are omitted.
checks.b_evidence_source: Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”; County Boundaries - Gary Thompson; Municipal Boundaries – Sarah Wray; SMAC Discussion and Approval; Simone Mountain; Stiles Mountain; Update on refresh of documents/tools on NC OneMap (www.nconemap.gov/pages/metadata); Adjustment to reflect the correct statutes and bring bylaws in line with those of other subcommittees; Join meeting; http://it.nc.gov/gicc
checks.b_evidence_output: The nested list items associated with these points are missing; the corresponding 'nested_list' values in the JSON are null.
checks.b_evidence_location: Framework+ Datasets (reading_order 9); Working Groups (reading_order 11); Other Business (reading_order 17)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There is a minor text omission in the first list item where the word 'minutes' is truncated, which slightly alters the specific meaning of the item.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. No text blocks or sections are repeated in the JSON output that are not also present in the original PDF.

The heading hierarchy is correctly implemented. The document title is an H1, major agenda sections are H2s, and the '2021 Meetings' sidebar is appropriately assigned an H3. There are no skips in heading levels.

The source document contains no tables. The '2021 Meetings' section, which is visually a block on the right, has been converted into a heading and paragraph, which is a suitable non-table structural representation for accessibility.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the agenda. The sidebar containing '2021 Meetings' is placed after the section it is visually adjacent to (Working Groups), which is an appropriate reading order for accessibility.

The conversion failed to capture any nested list structures. Multiple levels of sub-bullets present in the source document (under Elevation, Administrative/Governmental Units, NC Board on Geographic Names, Metadata Committee, and Other Business) are entirely missing from the output JSON. The lists are rendered as flat structures, resulting in significant structural and content loss.
checks.b_evidence_source: Framework+ Datasets section: 'Elevation - John Lay' followed by sub-bullet 'Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”'. Working Groups section: 'NC Board on Geographic Names' followed by 'SMAC Discussion and Approval' which has sub-items 'Simone Mountain' and 'Stiles Mountain'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list at reading_order 9 contains 'Elevation - John Lay' but no nested items. The list at reading_order 11 contains 'NC Board on Geographic Names - Cam McNutt' but no nested items or sub-nested items.

checks.a_analysis: Several hyperlinks present in the source document are missing from the output. The link 'Join meeting' is truncated from the end of the Web Conference line. The link 'minutes' is truncated from the end of the first agenda item. Additionally, a URL in the Metadata Committee sub-bullet is lost because the entire sub-bullet was omitted.
checks.b_evidence_source: Web Conference: Join meeting (link). Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes (link). www.nconemap.gov/pages/metadata (link).
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "**Location:** Meeting is through Web Conference only. No physical location.\n**Phone:** No call-in number. Audio provided through Web Conference link.\n**Web Conference:** " (reading_order 3). "text": "Approval of October 14, 2020 " (reading_order 6).
checks.b_evidence_location: Introductory paragraphs and list items at reading_order 3 and 6.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to maintain the visual hierarchy and structure of the source document. All nested sub-bullets (e.g., those under 'Elevation', 'Administrative/Governmental Units', 'NC Board on Geographic Names', 'Metadata Committee', and 'Other Business') are completely missing from the rendering. Furthermore, the 'Framework+ Datasets' list is visually fragmented, with the first bullet separated from the subsequent ones. The '2021 Meetings' section, which appears as a vertical list in the PDF, is compressed into a single line of text in the HTML. These discrepancies significantly alter the visual organization and flow of the information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Multiple nested sub-bullets (e.g., 'Updates on Contours and DEM QC and “sealing”', 'SMAC Discussion and Approval', 'Simone Mountain', etc.) and vertical list formatting for '2021 Meetings' and sub-items.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot lacks all nested sub-bullets, and the '2021 Meetings' dates are rendered as a single line: 'Wed 4/14 Wed 7/14 Wed 10/13'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically in the 'Framework+ Datasets', 'Working Groups', '2021 Meetings', and 'Other Business' sections.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; the information is presented in bulleted lists and text blocks. The layout of the meeting dates is not a formal table.
The source PDF contains no meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. Only text and decorative lines are present. The absence of decorative lines in the HTML is not a defect.

No body content duplication was found. The repetition of section headings in the sidebar Table of Contents and the main document body is an expected navigation feature for accessible design and does not constitute a defect.
